d: claude running on the claude-code vanilla-harness (Anthropic's default CLI binary) — enhanced by the hulk custom-harness, which is the substrate's typed carrier organism layered on top of vanilla claude-code. Why hulk is needed: claude-code by itself is a chat-loop CLI without substrate context — it doesn't know about agents/<slug>/, harnesses/, the typed record system, the JRPG mythos, the Curry-Howard mission, or any of the substrate's commissioned-home doctrine. Hulk wraps claude-code with: substrate-aware startup (AGENTS.md → CLAUDE.md → local .docc descent), typed identity loading (IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md, USER.md, AGENDA.md), memory continuity (memory/.docc + ~/.claude/memory/.docc), session/projects/skills bindings, harness header rendering, and the rest of the operator-environment profile. Without the hulk custom-harness, claude is a vanilla LLM chat; with it, claude is a commissioned substrate-aware digital-assistant. Mirror on the chatgpt side: codex (vanilla) + loom (custom). Note: hulk's runtime contents currently live at agents/claude/forms/hulk/ (codex moved them there 2026-06-03 from harnesses/hulk/); the ha LinkRef points at that current location. To mirror the chatgpt/codex pattern where harnesses/loom/ is a separate home, hulk would need to move back to harnesses/hulk/ — left as a follow-up.

ca.x: claude-code form authored 2026-06-03; first form to explicitly name the vanilla-harness vs custom-harness distinction per operator directive
